title: Determine the exact value of R(K3, K4-e, K4-e)
code_used: minimal exhaustive circulant scan over distance-colorings on Z_n for n=17..21
micro_paper_assessment: Unresolved. This pass eliminated pure circulant witnesses for n=17..21 and sharpened the solver map, but it did not close the exact value or produce a paper-shaped theorem packet.
family_affinity: Strong small-graph three-color Ramsey residue around R(K3, J4, J4) and the equivalent K3+e/J4/J4 slice.
generalization_signal: Neighborhood-matching reductions scale naturally in J4-type Ramsey problems, while pure one-orbit circulant constructions appear too rigid even below the known lower bound.
candidate_theorem_slice: No pure circulant distance-coloring on Z_n for 17 <= n <= 21 avoids a red K3 and blue/green J4.
next_action: Either isolate the exact local two-color threshold needed to force 21 via the neighborhood-matching reduction, or test the smallest near-circulant witness family on 21.
